intro: Dev-call intelligence - the running capture of what our daily calls cover (in-product, carriers/vendors, data & servicing), the goals we're driving, the blockers in the way, and items that still need a task. Auto-summarized from the Gemini call recap.
note: Populated by CIH from the Gemini dev-call recap email after each call. (The site is static - the on-page button loads the last CIH-published notes; CIH regenerates this from the newest Gemini email.)
goals: Lock the ILIA status model - split statuses into account, enrollment, and policy groups, map carrier-provided statuses (Allianz 14, National Life 8) one-to-one into those buckets, and keep underwriting/requirements under enrollment.
Finish the master-settlor data load - pull missing settlor emails where the settlor is not the insured, and overwrite trust names/EINs with verified Provident data so DBR can reprocess.
Close the illustrated-surrender-value gaps - run Tranche 67+ now and clean up the nine Tranche-66 policies still missing data.
Ship the Payments Center incrementally - land the new payment UI (annual baselines, audit history, rejection workflow) and sync its fields into Tranche Management and ePay for reporting.

Fix tranche mechanics - loosen ePay tranche-number locking so not-yet-in-force policies stay editable, restrict bulk moves to a single owner, and add a system Close-Tranche function that auto-advances prospects.

blockers.title: Carrier systematic-allocation-change form has a hard Sept 26 deadline
blockers.ask: Decide between the two handling options (stop new + terminate existing on the next monthly transfer vs. stop new + run existing to depletion) and complete client/agent outreach to move off monthly crediting before the form takes effect.

blockers.title: No agreed protocol for agents who move to competitors while keeping client business
blockers.ask: Management to formalize the offboarding protocol - the departing-agent case this call was handled ad hoc (access disabled, clients retained servicing, activity compiled for a possible non-compete). Codify the standard.

blockers.title: Irrevocable-trust handling needs a definitive spec before configuration
blockers.ask: Confirm the full requirements and DocuSign needs for revocable-to-irrevocable trust conversions, then implement a status/flag (not a new tranche) plus a Domo report formula to segregate affected clients for the bank.

blockers.title: DBR reprocessing still waiting on the master-settlor data load
blockers.ask: Pull the missing master-settlor emails (settlor not the insured) and overwrite trust names/EINs from the Provident list, then reprocess (Mohit / Olivia). No update this call.

needs_tasked.t: Irrevocable-trust identifier - add a status/flag field to ILIA and a Domo report formula to segregate revocable-to-irrevocable conversions (keep original tranche, no DB rebuild)
Automated Close-Tranche function - closing a tranche auto-advances remaining prospects to the next tranche, replacing manual bulk transfers
Restrict bulk tranche moves to a single designated owner to prevent premature/unauthorized transfers
Carrier systematic-allocation-change form - pick a handling option and build the client/agent outreach

latest_call.title: Dev Meeting
latest_call.lede: Servicing + platform call. Headlines: a departing agent's ILIA access is being disabled while their clients keep servicing access, with downloaded-document activity compiled for a possible non-compete review; the status-standardization project aligned on using carrier-provided statuses (Allianz's 14, National Life's 8) mapped one-to-one into three buckets - account, enrollment, and policy - with underwriting/requirements living under enrollment (NIWD-2070); National Life DocuSign completion certificates will mirror the Allianz auto-save process going forward (NIWD-2069); revocable-to-irrevocable trust conversions will be handled with a status flag / 'Tranche B' identifier rather than a new tranche or database rebuild; and ePay tranche-number locking that blocks edits on not-yet-in-force policies will be loosened (NIWD-2075), with bulk tranche moves restricted to one owner and a new system Close-Tranche function that auto-advances remaining prospects.

items.text: Status standardization: split ILIA statuses into three groups - account status (invited, opened email, created, disabled), enrollment status (product-enrollment steps, including underwriting and requirements), and policy status (high-level events like issued, surrendered, inactive).

Carrier-mapped policy statuses: adopt carrier-provided statuses one-to-one (Allianz 14, National Life 8) rather than a custom internal bucket, so agents keep familiar terminology; Jamin to update the planning doc with a visual breakdown of all status types to roll up into consolidated categories.
Admin status buckets: 'medically declined', 'client declined', and 'inactive' were added to the 'did not issue' bucket and treated as administrative prospect actions.

Irrevocable-trust identifier: add a status/flag to identify revocable-to-irrevocable trust conversions instead of building a new tranche; keep affected clients in their original tranche via a 'Tranche B' filter so funding dates and payment timelines stay synced.
Client document visibility: a client's personal-information-change documents were not showing; confirmed visible after a refresh during a screen-share, so no code change needed.

National Life DocuSign certificates: completion certificates are not being captured/named for National Life the way they are for Allianz (they save to the storage bucket but stay unnamed); future National Life applications will mirror the Allianz auto-save process. No retroactive backfill of historical records needed.

ePay tranche-number lock: policies are being locked when moved from one tranche to the next as an ePay payment posts near the deadline, which blocks edits; the lock is intended behavior once a payment is marked received, but it will be loosened so not-yet-in-force policies stay editable.
Tranche move controls: restrict bulk tranche moves to a single designated owner to prevent premature or unauthorized transfers; the three accounts locked in error have already been fixed.

Automated tranche closing: build a system Close-Tranche function that automatically advances remaining prospects to the next tranche, replacing manual bulk transfers; verify all in-force policies are correctly identified before closing historical tranches.

Irrevocable-trust reporting: flag clients converting to irrevocable status (e.g., moving out of Tranche 75) and segregate them via a Domo report formula rather than rebuilding database architecture; DocuSign handled as one-offs by Chris after the requirements are confirmed.
Status standardization uses carrier-provided statuses inside ILIA rather than a custom internal bucket system.
Underwriting/requirements statuses live under enrollment; policy statuses stay reserved for high-level events (issued, surrendered, inactive).
Future National Life applications mirror the Allianz certificate auto-save process; no retroactive update of historical records.
Revocable-to-irrevocable trust conversions handled via a status field / 'Tranche B' identifier that keeps the original tranche - no new tranche and no database rebuild.
A system-based Close-Tranche function replaces manual bulk transfers, and bulk moves are restricted to one designated owner.
A departing agent's account access is disabled while their clients retain servicing access; document-download activity is compiled for potential non-compete enforcement.

themes.name: Status Standardization (Account / Enrollment / Policy)
themes.latest: Aligned on splitting ILIA statuses into account, enrollment, and policy groups and mapping carrier-provided statuses one-to-one (Allianz 14, National Life 8) rather than a custom internal bucket. Underwriting/requirements sit under enrollment; policy status stays high-level (issued/surrendered/inactive). Jamin to add a visual breakdown of all status types to the planning doc.

themes.name: Agent Offboarding & Access Review
themes.latest: A departing agent (now with a competitor) is having ILIA account access disabled while their clients keep servicing access; the agent is directed to contact servicing for information. Downloaded-document activity was tracked via FullStory (screenshots can't be confirmed) and compiled for legal to weigh non-compete enforcement. Broader offboarding protocol still to be formalized with management.

themes.name: National Life DocuSign Certificates
themes.latest: Completion certificates aren't captured/named for National Life the way they are for Allianz (they save to the storage bucket but stay unnamed). Future National Life applications will mirror the Allianz auto-save process; no retroactive backfill needed.

themes.name: Irrevocable Trust 'Tranche B' Handling
themes.latest: Revocable-to-irrevocable trust conversions will be identified with a status/flag and a 'Tranche B' filter that keeps clients in their original tranche (funding dates and payment timelines stay synced), plus a Domo report formula to segregate them for the bank - avoiding a new tranche or DB rebuild. Requirements and DocuSign needs to be confirmed before configuration.

themes.name: ePay Tranche Lock / Not-In-Force Editing
themes.latest: Policies get locked when moved to the next tranche as an ePay payment posts near the deadline, blocking edits. The lock triggers when payment is marked received (intended behavior), but it will be loosened so policies that aren't yet in force stay editable.

themes.name: Automated Tranche Closing
themes.latest: Build a system Close-Tranche function that auto-advances remaining prospects to the next tranche, replacing manual bulk transfers and reducing errors. Bulk moves to be restricted to one designated owner; historical tranches to be closed once all in-force policies are verified.

themes.name: Great Wealth Transfer Review
themes.latest: Judy and Jamin to review the Great Wealth Transfer process together before presenting it to leadership; review scheduled immediately after this call.

themes.name: Systematic Allocation Change Form (Sept 26)
themes.latest: New carrier allocation-change form due Sept 26. Two options being weighed (stop new + terminate existing on next monthly transfer vs. stop new + run existing to depletion). Client/agent outreach planned to move off monthly crediting. No update this call.

themes.name: Allocation Combination Bug
themes.latest: A specific allocation combination is failing; traced to outdated rates in staging. Kyle to update staging rates, retest the sample payload, and comment on the task. No update this call.

themes.name: Kaizen Custom Design Wizard
themes.latest: Wizard UI to drop the contribution-vs-death-benefit page in favor of a direct death-benefit entry field; confirmed ready for implementation. No update this call.

themes.name: Employer Contribution UI
themes.latest: Estimator to display employer contributions in a simplified format; approved into the sprint. No update this call.

themes.name: Estimator Table Rating Support
themes.latest: Bug believed resolved by recent changes; awaiting final confirmation before the task can be closed. No update this call.

themes.name: Low Point Data (NLG / Allianz)
themes.latest: Low-point values differ from bank data on National Life and Allianz illustrations; renewal data handled differently from first-year. Kyle testing against sample illustrations, with the option to build a scrap report. No update this call.

themes.name: Action Item Notes on Illustration Changes
themes.latest: Add a notes field when agents/clients request illustration changes so requests are trackable (today they land in the illustration inbox). No update this call.

themes.name: Estimator to Agent-Portal Invitations
themes.latest: 'Create Invitation' CTA from Estimator results, dependent on adding the standalone Estimator to the agent portal (NIWD-1792); tasks to be linked. No update this call.

themes.name: Payments Center to ePay / Notifications Sync
themes.latest: Sync monthly installment due-date changes to ePay transaction requests and existing notification schedules; moved into the new sprint. No update this call.

themes.name: GWT Gifter Portal - Verify Label
themes.latest: Removal of the 'Verify' status label; task likely to be deleted pending final input. No update this call.

themes.name: Bank Dropbox / Client File Uploads
themes.latest: Uploading trunch + client files to the bank Dropbox/Egnyte; document locations still being defined. Final docs go to the carrier when sent per client; trunch-specific docs pull at the trunch-master stage. No update this call.

themes.name: Missing Statements & Scraper Data Gaps
themes.latest: Annual-review statement-data task under review; batch request to National Life and date-logic fix still in play. No update this call.

themes.name: DBR / Master-Settlor Data Load
themes.latest: Master-settlor emails to be pulled where the settlor is not the insured, and trust names/EINs overwritten from the verified Provident list; DBR task updated to require emails so it can reprocess. No update this call.

themes.name: Payments Center & Tranche Mgmt Integration
themes.latest: New payment UI - annual baselines, expectation statuses, rejection workflow with supporting-doc requests, and full audit history - to be mapped into Tranche Management and released in stages. No update this call.

themes.name: Illustrated Surrender Values (Tranche 66/67)
themes.latest: Tranche 66 pulled with 9 of 94 missing data; approved to run Tranche 67+ while the Tranche-66 gaps are cleaned up. No update this call.
